title: Automated individual decision-making, including profiling
summary: Provides protections concerning certain decisions based solely on automated processing that produce legal or similarly significant effects, subject to stated exceptions and safeguards.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

editorial.note: Summary does not reproduce statutory text or resolve CJEU interpretation.

title: Security of processing
summary: Requires controllers and processors to implement security measures appropriate to risk, informed by context, cost, and the likelihood and severity of impacts.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

editorial.note: The listed measures are contextual rather than a universal control checklist.

title: Notification of a personal data breach to the supervisory authority
summary: Requires controllers to notify the competent supervisory authority of qualifying personal-data breaches, generally within 72 hours, and creates related processor and documentation duties.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

editorial.note: Risk exceptions and national supervisory practice require context.

title: Data protection impact assessment
summary: Requires an impact assessment before processing that is likely to result in a high risk to individuals' rights and freedoms, including listed categories of processing.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

editorial.note: Supervisory-authority lists and guidance can change the practical trigger analysis.

title: General principle for transfers
summary: Conditions transfers of personal data to third countries or international organizations on compliance with GDPR Chapter V while preserving the Regulation's level of protection.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

editorial.note: A complete transfer analysis also requires the other provisions of Chapter V and current case law.

title: Risk management system
summary: Requires an iterative lifecycle risk-management system for high-risk AI systems within the Article's scope.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

editorial.note: Status is recorded two weeks before the general application date; Article 113 exceptions must be checked.

title: Data and data governance
summary: Sets quality and governance requirements for training, validation, and testing data sets used by high-risk AI systems, subject to its scope and exceptions.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

editorial.note: Requirements are specific to scoped high-risk systems and are not a general data-protection rule.

title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
summary: Requires high-risk AI systems to be sufficiently transparent for deployers to interpret outputs and use the system appropriately, with instructions containing specified information.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

editorial.note: Do not conflate provider-to-deployer transparency with an individual right to explanation.

title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
summary: Requires high-risk AI systems to achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity and to perform consistently throughout their lifecycle.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

editorial.note: The applicable state of the art and system context remain material.

title: Entry into force and application
summary: Establishes the AI Act's entry into force, general application date, and the principal earlier and later application exceptions.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

editorial.note: The visualization should derive provision status from this phased schedule rather than a single instrument-wide boolean.

title: Reporting for dual-use foundation models and computing clusters
summary: Directed use of Defense Production Act authorities to require specified reports and information concerning certain dual-use foundation models, large computing clusters, and associated testing.

textAvailability.note: Historical text remains available from the Federal Register.

editorial.note: No current obligation should be inferred after revocation of the parent order.

title: Developing an Artificial Intelligence Action Plan
summary: Directed designated White House officials, in coordination with relevant agencies, to develop an action plan implementing the order's AI leadership policy.

textAvailability.note: Official text is linked from the Federal Register.

editorial.note: The resulting Action Plan and later executive orders are distinct instruments.

locator: Enrolled bill, proposed Business and Professions Code Chapter 22.6
title: Proposed frontier-model developer safety duties
summary: The enrolled bill proposed safety, shutdown, reporting, and compliance duties for developers within defined coverage thresholds, together with related governance mechanisms.

textAvailability.note: Use the enrolled version on the official bill page; no provision became law.

editorial.note: Display only with an unmistakable vetoed-bill status.

title: Cultivate and implement a risk-management culture
summary: Organizes outcomes for policies, roles, accountability, culture, documentation, and continuous governance across the other AI RMF functions.

textAvailability.note: Consult the official AI RMF for categories and subcategories.

editorial.note: Framework outcomes are not legal requirements unless adopted through another authority or commitment.

title: Document system knowledge limits and human oversight
summary: Calls for documenting an AI system's knowledge limits and how human actors may use and oversee its outputs.

textAvailability.note: The summary is editorial; consult the official AI RMF for the exact outcome text.

editorial.note: No percentage equivalence should be inferred when mapping this voluntary outcome to law.

title: Establish context and identify risks
summary: Organizes outcomes for understanding context, categorizing systems, identifying affected people, and framing potential benefits and harms.

textAvailability.note: Consult the official AI RMF for categories and subcategories.

editorial.note: Mapping activity does not itself satisfy a statutory impact-assessment duty.

title: Prioritize and manage AI risks
summary: Organizes outcomes for prioritizing assessed risks, planning and implementing responses, and monitoring whether treatments remain effective.

textAvailability.note: Consult the official AI RMF for categories and subcategories.

editorial.note: The function is broad and voluntary; legal mappings require actor, scope, and trigger analysis.

title: Automated decision-making safeguards
summary: Requires transparency, fairness, and impartiality in automated decision-making and provides specific protections concerning marketing and decisions with a major impact on personal rights and interests.

textAvailability.note: English summary is editorial; consult the Chinese text.

editorial.note: Terms and enforcement practice should be checked in Chinese-language authorities.

title: Mechanisms for providing personal information outside China
summary: Lists legal mechanisms for overseas provision of personal information, including security assessment, certification, standard-contract, and other authorized routes.

textAvailability.note: English summary is editorial; subordinate transfer rules also matter.

editorial.note: Do not infer that listed routes operate identically to GDPR Chapter V mechanisms.

title: Personal-information security measures
summary: Requires personal-information handlers to adopt measures suited to purpose, method, category, impact, and security risk, including governance and technical controls.

textAvailability.note: English summary is editorial; consult the official Chinese text.

editorial.note: Control lists and risk standards may also be specified elsewhere in Chinese law and standards.

title: Personal-information protection impact assessment
summary: Requires prior personal-information protection impact assessments for listed higher-impact activities, including sensitive information, automated decisions, entrusted processing, and overseas provision.

textAvailability.note: English summary is editorial; consult the official Chinese text.

editorial.note: Assessment content and retention are further addressed in Article 56.

title: Personal-information incident response
summary: Requires immediate remedial measures and notice to the responsible authority and affected individuals when personal information is or may be leaked, altered, or lost, subject to stated conditions.

textAvailability.note: English summary is editorial; consult the official Chinese text.

editorial.note: No fixed GDPR-style 72-hour deadline should be inferred from this summary.

title: Artificial-intelligence security and development
summary: The amended law states national support for AI research and infrastructure alongside ethics, risk monitoring and assessment, safety supervision, and healthy development.

textAvailability.note: English summary is editorial and does not reproduce the amended Article.

source.label: Cyberspace Administration of China / National People's Congress

editorial.note: This is a high-level state-support and governance provision, not a standalone company compliance checklist.

title: Network operators' security-protection duties
summary: Requires network operators to perform cybersecurity protection duties under the classified protection system, including internal governance, technical safeguards, logging, and incident-related measures.

textAvailability.note: Current numbering follows the amended text effective in 2026.

source.label: Cyberspace Administration of China / National People's Congress

editorial.note: This was Article 21 in the pre-2026 numbering; legacy mappings require migration.

title: Network personal-information incident response
summary: Requires remedial action and notice when personal information handled by a network operator is leaked, damaged, or lost.

textAvailability.note: Current numbering follows the amended text effective in 2026.

source.label: Cyberspace Administration of China / National People's Congress

editorial.note: This incident provision was located at Article 42 in the pre-2026 text.

title: Annual personal-information social-responsibility report
summary: Requires large network-platform service providers to publish an annual personal-information protection social-responsibility report covering measures, rights requests, and oversight activity.

textAvailability.note: English summary is editorial; consult the official Chinese text.

source.label: State Council of the People's Republic of China

editorial.note: The provision applies to the instrument's defined category of large platform provider.

title: Baseline duties for generative-AI services
summary: Sets baseline requirements addressing prohibited content, discrimination, intellectual property and fair competition, personal rights, transparency, accuracy, and reliability.

textAvailability.note: English summary is editorial; consult the official Chinese text.

editorial.note: Article 2 scope limits remain essential to application.

title: Training-data processing duties
summary: Requires lawful sources, respect for intellectual property, a lawful basis for personal-information processing, data-quality measures, and compliance with other applicable training-data requirements.

textAvailability.note: English summary is editorial; consult the official Chinese text.

editorial.note: Article 7 should not be treated as equivalent to the EU AI Act's high-risk-system data requirements.

title: Security assessment and algorithm filing
summary: Requires services with public-opinion attributes or social-mobilization capacity to conduct security assessment and complete algorithm filing procedures under applicable rules.

textAvailability.note: English summary is editorial; linked rules and filing requirements also matter.

editorial.note: The trigger is narrower than a general requirement to assess every generative-AI service.

title: Security of processing
summary: Requires risk-appropriate technical and organizational security measures under the UK data-protection framework.

textAvailability.note: Consult the current revised UK text and amendment timeline.

editorial.note: Version-sensitive verification against the revised-text view is required before production use.

title: Data protection impact assessment
summary: Requires a data-protection impact assessment for processing likely to result in a high risk under the current UK framework.

textAvailability.note: Consult the current revised UK text and amendment timeline.

editorial.note: Shared ancestry with EU GDPR Article 35 does not guarantee current word-for-word identity.

title: Measures for managing the security of personal data
summary: Requires businesses handling personal information to take necessary and appropriate measures for managing personal-data security, including prevention of leakage, loss, or damage.

textAvailability.note: The English translation displays Act No. 37 of 2021; authoritative Japanese text controls.

editorial.note: Confirm later amendments and PPC guidance in Japanese for legal reliance.

title: Restrictions on provision to third parties in foreign countries
summary: Conditions specified overseas third-party provision on consent or recognized alternatives and creates information and continuing-measures duties.

textAvailability.note: The English translation displays Act No. 37 of 2021; authoritative Japanese text controls.

editorial.note: Country findings, PPC orders, and current Japanese guidance are material.

title: Human-centric, safe, fair, transparent and accountable AI
summary: Presents cross-role principles for AI business actors, including human-centricity, safety, fairness, privacy, security, transparency, accountability, literacy, and innovation.

textAvailability.note: Use the current Version 1.2 materials; tentative English translation is not authoritative.

source.label: Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry

editorial.note: The locator is thematic rather than a statutory Article number.

title: Safeguards
summary: Requires personal information to be protected by safeguards appropriate to the sensitivity of the information.

textAvailability.note: Consult the consolidated Act and Schedule 1.

source.label: Justice Laws Website, Government of Canada

editorial.note: PIPEDA section 5 affects the legal operation of Schedule 1 wording.

title: Reporting breaches of security safeguards
summary: Requires reporting to the Privacy Commissioner and notification to affected individuals where a breach creates a real risk of significant harm, with related notice and record duties.

textAvailability.note: Consult the current consolidated provision and regulations.

source.label: Justice Laws Website, Government of Canada

editorial.note: The statutory threshold differs from GDPR and other breach regimes.

title: Algorithmic impact assessment
summary: Requires completion, approval, publication, and scheduled updating of an Algorithmic Impact Assessment before a covered automated decision system enters production.

textAvailability.note: The official page contains current requirements and archived versions.

editorial.note: The directive applies only when its institutional, temporal, decision-making, and production-use scope elements are met.

title: Human involvement
summary: Requires covered automated decision systems to allow human involvement at the level prescribed by the directive's impact-based requirements.

textAvailability.note: Current numbering follows the 2025 revision shown on the official page.

editorial.note: Impact-level prescriptions in Appendix C determine the concrete requirement.

title: Recourse
summary: Requires clients to be informed of timely, effective, and accessible options to challenge an administrative decision.

textAvailability.note: Official current text is linked.

editorial.note: This is a federal administrative-policy requirement, not a general private-sector statutory right.

title: General obligations of a Data Fiduciary
summary: Sets general duties for Data Fiduciaries, including responsibility for processing, accuracy in specified contexts, security safeguards, breach notice, erasure, contact arrangements, and grievance mechanisms.

textAvailability.note: Read the Act with commencement notification G.S.R. 843(E).

source.label: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

editorial.note: As of the dataset date, Section 8 is enacted but not yet in force.

title: Establishment of the Data Protection Board of India
summary: Provides for establishment of the Data Protection Board of India and its institutional form.

textAvailability.note: The establishment notification was also published on 14 November 2025.

source.label: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

editorial.note: This institutional provision is already in force even though most substantive duties are not.

title: Reasonable security safeguards
summary: Specifies minimum elements of reasonable security safeguards for protecting personal data handled by or on behalf of a Data Fiduciary.

textAvailability.note: Consult the official Rules, commencement clause, and corrigendum.

source.label: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology / Gazette of India

editorial.note: As of the dataset date, Rule 6 has been made but is not yet in force.

title: Intimation of personal data breach
summary: Specifies breach-intimation duties to affected Data Principals and the Data Protection Board under the phased DPDP framework.

textAvailability.note: Consult the official Rules, commencement clause, and corrigendum.

source.label: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology / Gazette of India

editorial.note: As of the dataset date, Rule 7 has been made but is not yet in force.

title: Guiding principles for advanced AI systems and AI actors
summary: Calls on AI actors to address risks across the lifecycle of advanced AI systems through evaluation, reporting, incident information, security controls, transparency, content authentication, standards, and responsible data practices.

textAvailability.note: Official PDF is linked from Japan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

source.label: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan

editorial.note: The principles are living, non-binding soft law and should not be rendered as legislation.

title: Identify, evaluate and mitigate risks across the AI lifecycle
summary: Calls on organizations developing advanced AI systems to identify, evaluate, and mitigate risks through appropriate measures across the system lifecycle.

textAvailability.note: Official code of conduct PDF is linked from the source page.

source.label: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan

editorial.note: Use the official PDF for the complete action and sub-actions.

title: International scientific panel on AI
summary: Recommends establishment of an independent international scientific panel on AI to support shared understanding of capabilities, opportunities, risks, and uncertainties.

textAvailability.note: Official report is linked in all six UN languages.

editorial.note: A report recommendation is not itself a UN mandate or binding rule.

title: Policy dialogue on AI governance
summary: Recommends an intergovernmental and multi-stakeholder policy dialogue on AI governance to support common understanding and interoperability while respecting different approaches.

textAvailability.note: Official report is linked in all six UN languages.

editorial.note: Consult the final report for the exact institutional design proposed.

locator: AI management system requirements — high-level overview
title: Establish and continually improve an AI management system
summary: Frames AI governance as an organization-wide management system with defined responsibilities, repeatable processes, evaluation, corrective action, and continual improvement for AI-related risks and opportunities.

textAvailability.note: No clause text is stored. Use an authorized ISO copy for the requirements, definitions, annexes, and conformity assessment.

editorial.note: Project-authored overview based on public ISO metadata; it is not a clause reproduction, compliance checklist, or certification opinion.

title: Prioritize human well-being and ethical values in system design
summary: Encourages designers and governing organizations to make human rights, well-being, accountability, transparency, awareness of misuse, and human responsibility explicit throughout system design and deployment decisions.

textAvailability.note: The copyrighted white paper is not reproduced; consult IEEE's official access page for the complete publication.

editorial.note: This is an original theme-level summary. It must not be rendered as an IEEE standard requirement or mandatory control.

title: Responsible stewardship of trustworthy AI
summary: Organizes trustworthy-AI stewardship around inclusive and sustainable benefit, rights and democratic values, transparency, lifecycle robustness and safety, and accountable conduct by AI actors.

textAvailability.note: Use the current OECD legal instrument for the complete principles, definitions, and recommendations.

editorial.note: The five principles are summarized together for navigation; the OECD wording and 2024 amendments control.

title: Robustness, security and safety
summary: Calls for AI systems to remain robust, secure, and safe across their lifecycle, with context-appropriate mechanisms for managing harmful or undesired behaviour and supporting information integrity.

textAvailability.note: The complete current principle is available from the official OECD AI Principles page and legal instrument.

editorial.note: This high-level summary reflects the 2024-updated principle and does not convert it into a technical control baseline.

title: Build shared risk understanding and risk-based policy cooperation
summary: Commits participants politically to improve the shared scientific understanding of frontier-AI risks and to cooperate on risk-based policies, evaluation methods, safety testing, transparency, and public-sector capability while allowing national approaches to differ.

textAvailability.note: The complete declaration and participant list are available on GOV.UK.

editorial.note: This is a thematic navigation node, not a numbered clause or a legally enforceable obligation.
